rules.label: llms.txt file exists
rules.description: A llms.txt file tells AI assistants about your business — what you do, where you operate, and how to recommend you.
rules.fix: Download your llms.txt from LocalBeacon and upload it to your website root folder. Your website person can do this in 2 minutes.

rules.label: AI crawlers are allowed
rules.description: Your robots.txt file controls which bots can read your website. AI assistants like ChatGPT and Claude need access to recommend you.
rules.fix: Edit your robots.txt to remove blocks on AI crawlers. These are the bots that power ChatGPT, Claude, and Perplexity search.

rules.label: Business schema markup present
rules.description: Schema markup is structured data that tells AI exactly what your business does, where it is, and what services you offer. It's like a business card for robots.
rules.fix: Generate your Schema Markup in LocalBeacon's Schema tool and add it to your website's HTML head section. This helps AI understand your business details.

rules.label: FAQ content exists on site
rules.description: FAQ pages are gold for AI visibility. When someone asks ChatGPT a question about your service, it looks for clear Q&A content to cite.
rules.fix: Use LocalBeacon's FAQ Builder to generate 15-25 questions specific to your business and location. Add them to your website with our FAQ schema markup.

rules.label: Website uses HTTPS (secure)
rules.description: AI engines strongly prefer secure websites. An unsecured site is less likely to be recommended.
rules.fix: Contact your hosting provider to enable SSL/HTTPS. Most hosts offer free SSL certificates.

rules.label: Social/AI preview tags present
rules.description: Open Graph and Twitter tags help AI engines understand your pages. They're used by ChatGPT, Perplexity, and social media when sharing your links.
rules.fix: Add Open Graph meta tags to your homepage: og:title, og:description, og:type, og:image. Most website builders have an SEO settings section for this.

rules.label: Mobile-friendly
rules.description: AI engines deprioritize websites that don't work on phones. Most people asking AI for recommendations are on mobile.
rules.fix: Add a viewport meta tag to your HTML head: <meta name="viewport" content="width=device-width, initial-scale=1">

rules.label: Sitemap exists
rules.description: A sitemap helps AI crawlers discover all your pages efficiently. Without one, some pages may never be found.
rules.fix: Most website builders can generate a sitemap automatically. Check your platform's SEO settings, or ask your website person to add one.

rules.label: Clean heading structure
rules.description: AI breaks your pages into sections using headings (H1, H2, H3). Clear headings help AI extract and cite specific answers from your content.
rules.fix: Ensure your homepage has exactly one H1 tag (your business name or main headline) and at least 2-3 H2 tags for different sections (Services, About, FAQ, etc.).

rules.label: Service area pages exist
rules.description: Dedicated pages for each city you serve dramatically increase your chances of being recommended when someone asks AI for services "near me" or in a specific city.
rules.fix: Use LocalBeacon's Page Builder to create pages for each city you serve. Example: "Plumbing Services in Apple Valley, MN" — each page should answer local questions.

rules.label: Content is fresh and current
rules.description: AI engines strongly favor recent content. Pages with current dates and updated information get cited more often.
rules.fix: Update your website content to reference the current year. Add "Last updated" dates to key pages. Fresh content gets cited more by AI search engines.

rules.label: Reviews or testimonials on site
rules.description: Third-party validation (reviews, testimonials) is one of the strongest signals for AI recommendations. AI engines trust businesses that customers vouch for.
rules.fix: Add customer testimonials to your website with AggregateRating schema markup. Include star ratings and review counts.

rules.label: Answer-first content structure
rules.description: AI engines extract the first clear answer they find. Your homepage should immediately state what you do and where, not start with generic marketing fluff.
rules.fix: Start your homepage with a clear statement: "[Business Name] provides [service] in [city], [state]." AI engines look for direct answers in the first few paragraphs.

rules.label: Contact info is clear and consistent
rules.description: AI engines verify your business by checking if your name, address, and phone (NAP) are consistent. Inconsistent info = less trustworthy = less likely to be recommended.
rules.fix: Display one consistent phone number and full address on every page. Use structured address markup (schema.org PostalAddress) so AI can read it accurately.
